description:
Scott and Wes unpack WebMCP, a new standard that lets AI interact with websites through structured tools instead of slow, bot-style clicking. They demo it, debate imperative vs declarative APIs, and share their hottest take: this might be the web’s real AI moment. 

To get access you can join the Chrome Early Preview Program. I think you can just turn on the WebMCP flag in about:flags though.  https://developer.chrome.com/docs/ai/join-epp

The docs are here: https://docs.google.com/document/d/1rtU1fRPS0bMqd9abMG_hc6K9OAI6soUy3Kh00toAgyk/edit?tab=t.0

And the Chrome extension: https://chromewebstore.google.com/detail/model-context-tool-inspec/gbpdfapgefenggkahomfgkhfehlcenpd

🔥 Be the ~18,300th person to join our super tasty newsletter https://bit.ly/syntax_snackpack

Show Notes

01:07 Understanding WebMCP Functionality.
03:06 Interacting with AI through WebMCP.
06:49 WebMCP browser integration.
08:25 Brought to you by Sentry.io.
08:49 Benefits of WebMCP.
11:51 Token efficiency.
13:02 My biggest questions.
14:13 My take on this tech.
